mechanism_narrative: A falling federal funds rate transmits to equity prices through lower discount rates and easier financial conditions, lifting S&P 500 returns over the next month when policy is actively easing. The signal is strongest when inflation is not accelerating at the same time.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive on average after months with falling FEDFUNDS, or if the sign flips and returns are negative in more than 55% of easing episodes over a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in unemployment above a mild threshold signals weakening household income and business demand, which tends to pressure S&P 500 returns lower over the next month. The mechanism activates when the labor market is no longer stable and joblessness is moving up from recent levels.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not produce negative 20-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the average forward return after a 0.3pp three-month UNRATE rise is nonnegative across any 2-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth and employment growth jointly confirm broad demand strength, which supports earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect is not decomposable into either series alone because both are needed to distinguish real demand expansion from temporary inventory or hiring noise.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition on RSAFS and PAYEMS does not outperform either series alone in out-of-sample Rank IC, or if positive 20-day SPX returns are not more frequent when both measures improve together.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in industrial production indicates stronger real activity and better revenue realization for cyclical firms, which should push S&P 500 returns higher over the next month. The signal is strongest when output growth is positive rather than merely stable.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises above its prior 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if triggered periods do not show higher mean 20-day SPX returns than untriggered periods, or if the effect disappears in any rolling 3-year window with Rank IC below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained housing-starts slowdown signals weaker forward demand, tighter credit sensitivity, and lower cyclical earnings expectations, which should translate into negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The mechanism is slow-moving and should be more visible at a 60-day horizon than at a 5-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward SPX returns are not negative on average after housing-starts declines below the 6-month average, or if the trigger produces no better-than-random hit rate over a 4-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A high federal funds rate becomes bearish only when inflation is still elevated, because real financing costs stay restrictive and equity duration is penalized. This interaction should predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when policy is tight and inflation remains sticky.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS above 4.5% and CPIAUCSL rising over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the joint high-rate/high-inflation state does not produce negative 20-day SPX returns more often than low-rate or disinflation states, or if the interaction term is insignificant in a rolling walk-forward regression.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll growth combined with low unemployment marks a resilient labor market that supports consumption and earnings revisions, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is strongest when payrolls are still growing while unemployment remains near recent lows.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined PAYEMS/UNRATE condition does not outperform single-series signals, or if positive 20-day SPX returns are not concentrated in episodes with rising payrolls and stable-to-lower unemployment.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP acceleration should support equity returns only in a post-shock recovery, when output is rebounding from weak levels and investors reprice earnings upward. The signal is slower-moving and should be evaluated over a quarter rather than a few days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 accelerates versus the prior quarter
failure_condition: Reject if quarterly GDP acceleration does not correspond to positive 60-day forward SPX returns in recovery regimes, or if the effect vanishes after controlling for baseline market drift in any rolling 5-year sample.
